# Xcode Hotkeys and Quick Actions for VSCode Developers

Welcome. If you are accustomed to VSCode, your muscle memory will need minor adjustments for Xcode. 

The following tables map standard VSCode hotkeys to their exact Xcode equivalents. Use the visual references to locate the keys quickly.

### Key Legend
- `⌘` : Command
- `⇧` : Shift
- `⌥` : Option
- `⌃` : Control

## Core Navigation

| Objective | VSCode | Xcode | Note |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| **Find/Open a File** | `⌘` `P` | `⇧` `⌘` `O` | Xcode calls this "Open Quickly". |
| **Command Palette** | `⇧` `⌘` `P` | `⇧` `⌘` `A` | Xcode calls this "Quick Actions". Type to find application commands. |
| **Toggle Left Sidebar** | `⌘` `B` | `⌘` `0` | Toggles the "Navigator" panel. |
| **Toggle Right Sidebar** | - | `⌥` `⌘` `0` | Toggles the "Inspectors" panel. |
| **Toggle Bottom Panel** | `⌘` `J` | `⇧` `⌘` `Y` | Toggles the "Debug Area" (console). |

## Search and Editing

| Objective | VSCode | Xcode | Note |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| **Save File** | `⌘` `S` | `⌘` `S` | Identical behavior. |
| **Find Text in File** | `⌘` `F` | `⌘` `F` | Identical behavior. |
| **Find Text in Project** | `⇧` `⌘` `F` | `⇧` `⌘` `F` | Identical behavior. Opens the Find Navigator. |
| **Toggle Comment** | `⌘` `/` | `⌘` `/` | Identical behavior. |
| **Format Code** | `⇧` `⌥` `F` | `⌃` `I` | Select text first (e.g., `⌘` `A`), then `⌃` `I` to "Re-Indent". |
| **Rename Symbol** | `F2` | `⌃` `⌘` `E` | "Edit All in Scope". Safely renames all instances. |
| **Go to Definition** | `F12` or `⌘` Click | `⌃` `⌘` `J` | Alternatively, `⌘` Click on a symbol to open a context menu. |

## The Quick Action Menu (`⇧` `⌘` `A`)

In VSCode, the Command Palette (`⇧` `⌘` `P`) is the primary way to execute tasks. In Xcode, Quick Actions (`⇧` `⌘` `A`) serves the exact same purpose. It provides a visual search overlay for all available Xcode menu commands. 

## The Action Menu (`⌘` Click)

In Xcode, holding `⌘` and clicking on any code symbol (struct, variable, view) opens a visual Action Menu. This is a primary visual interaction method in Xcode for refactoring, extracting subviews, or jumping to definitions.
